title: Essential Oils for Cold and Flu: 11 Herbal Remedies

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole in <built-in method capitalize of str object at 0x7933f60c3030> Ginkgo biloba is good for colds because it has natural decongestant properties that help reduce inflammation and mucus production in the nose and throat, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.
Pinene helps relieve cold symptoms by opening airways and reducing inflammation, making it easier to breathe and recover.
Eucalyptol is good for colds because it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion and coughs.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oil is often used to help relieve cold symptoms. The oil is extracted from the leaves of the Eucalyptus globulus tree, which is native to Australia. When inhaled, the oil helps to clear congestion and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses. This can make it easier to breathe and help ease a sore throat. The oil's decongestant properties also help to loosen and remove mucus, making it a popular remedy for coughs and runny noses. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus oil has antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent the spread of infections. When used in a humidifier or applied topically to the chest, it may also help to reduce fever and promote relaxation.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils that help with colds, you'll need a few basic materials. First, you'll need fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, which can be found in health food stores or online. Next, you'll need a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a small saucepan for distillation. To start, combine 2 cups of Ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 cup of carrier oil in the saucepan, and heat it gently over low heat for 2-3 hours. The heat will help release the oils from the leaves and infuse them into the carrier oil. As the mixture cools, you'll need to strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the leaves, and then store the oil in a dark glass bottle. You can use this oil by applying a few drops to your skin, inhaling it, or adding it to a warm bath to help relieve cold symptoms like congestion and coughs.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for colds

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for colds because it has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help fight off the infection and reduce the swelling in the throat and nasal passages.
Carvacrol in <built-in method capitalize of str object at 0x7933f60c3030> Ginkgo biloba helps fight colds by reducing inflammation, killing germs, and easing congestion in the throat and nose.
Rosmarinic acid is good for colds because it has strong antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and fight off viral infections, making it easier to recover from a cold.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oil, also known as thyme oil, is a natural remedy for colds. It contains compounds called thymol and carvacrol, which have strong antiviral and antibacterial properties. When inhaled, thyme oil helps to clear mucus from the nose and sinuses, making it easier to breathe. It also reduces inflammation and soothes a sore throat. Thyme oil's antimicrobial properties help to fight off the underlying infection that causes a cold, reducing its severity and duration. Additionally, thyme oil has decongestant properties, which help to relieve congestion and sinus pressure. When used in a humidifier or diffuser, thyme oil can help to ease cold symptoms and promote a faster recovery.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil that helps with colds, start by harvesting fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted supplier. Next, you'll need a clean glass container with a lid, a carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil, and a dark glass bottle for storing the final product. Begin by drying the Ginkgo biloba leaves completely, either air-drying them or using a food dehydrator on the lowest temperature setting. Then, combine 1 part dried Ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 parts carrier oil in the glass container, and let it steep in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container occasionally. After the infusion process, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, discarding the solids. Finally, store the Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil in a cool, dark place and use it as needed to help alleviate cold symptoms, by adding a few drops to a diffuser or mixing it with a carrier oil for topical use.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal essential oils for colds

Caffeic acid is good for colds because it has strong antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and fight off viral infections in the body.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oil is good for colds because it helps to boost the immune system. The plant's roots and flowers have been used for centuries in traditional medicine to fight off infections and reduce inflammation. When inhaled, the oil's active compounds, such as cichoric acid and alkylamides, can help to reduce congestion and ease coughs. These compounds also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe a sore throat and reduce the severity of cold symptoms. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea essential oil can help to reduce the production of mucus, making it easier to breathe and clear out the airways. By supporting the immune system and reducing inflammation, Echinacea purpurea essential oil may help to alleviate the discomfort and discomfort of a cold.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils that can help with colds, you will need some basic equipment and ingredients. First, you will need some Ginkgo biloba leaves or seeds, which can be found at most health food stores or online. Next, you will need a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, and a clean glass jar with a lid. To make the oil, start by washing the Ginkgo biloba leaves or seeds in cold water and drying them thoroughly with a paper towel. Then, place the dried leaves or seeds in the glass jar and pour the carrier oil over them, making sure they are completely covered. Next, place the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the oil infuse into the Ginkgo biloba. After 2-3 weeks, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, and discard the solids. The resulting oil can be used in aromatherapy to help ease cold symptoms and promote recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils for colds

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for colds because it has strong antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the body and prevent the growth of the virus that causes colds.
Carnosic acid is good for colds because it has powerful antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and fight off the viruses that cause colds.
Quercetin is good for colds because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and recover from a cold.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil, also known as rosemary oil, is a natural remedy for colds. It has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nose and sinuses. This makes it easier to breathe and can help to relieve a sore throat. Rosemary oil also has antimicrobial properties that help to fight off the underlying infection that causes a cold. It contains compounds like camphor and borneol that have decongestant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways. When inhaled, rosemary oil can also help to open up air passages and improve respiratory function, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, rosemary oil has antiviral properties that help to combat the virus that causes a cold, helping to speed up the recovery process.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil that helps with colds, you will need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. First, dry the Ginkgo biloba leaves completely in a warm, dry place or using a food dehydrator. Once dry, chop the leaves finely and place them in a clean glass jar. Next, fill a saucepan with water and bring it to a boil, then reduce the heat and place the jar with the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the water. This process is called steam distillation, and it helps to release the beneficial compounds from the leaves into the air. As the steam rises, it will carry the fragrance and healing properties of the Ginkgo biloba into the air, and you can collect this steam by placing a clean cloth or paper towel over the jar. Finally, mix the collected steam with a carrier oil in a ratio of about 1 part Ginkgo biloba to 10 parts carrier oil, and store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle. This Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil can be used to help relieve cold symptoms, such as congestion and coughs, by inhaling it or applying it topically to the chest.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for colds

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole is good for colds because it helps to open up airways, reduce inflammation, and loosen mucus, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.
Catechin helps to reduce the severity of colds by acting as a potent antioxidant, which fights off free radicals in the body and helps to prevent the multiplication of the viruses that cause colds.
Linalool is good for colds because it has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce congestion, soothe a sore throat, and relieve other cold symptoms.
Terpinen-4-ol helps fight colds by reducing inflammation and killing germs in the body, making it easier for the immune system to recover from illness.
Quercetin is good for colds because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nose and sinuses.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil, also known as tea tree oil, is a natural remedy for colds. It has strong antimicrobial properties, which help fight against the bacteria and viruses that cause colds. When inhaled, it can ease congestion and open up airways, making it easier to breathe. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce swelling and pain in the nose and throat. Additionally, tea tree oil has antiseptic properties that help prevent the spread of infection and promote healing in the body. It can be used in a diffuser to create a soothing atmosphere, or added to a warm bath to help relax and reduce symptoms of a cold.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils for cold relief, you'll need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, dry and clean 1-2 cups of Ginkgo biloba leaves, and then chop them into small pieces. Next, place the chopped leaves in a clean glass jar and cover them with 2-3 cups of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil. Now, seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the leaves infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, and discard the solids. Finally, transfer the infused oil to a small bottle and label it. Ginkgo biloba essential oil has been traditionally used to help alleviate cold symptoms, so you can use a few drops of it in a diffuser or apply it topically to your chest and nose to help ease congestion.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for colds

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Menthol is good for colds because it helps to thin out mucus, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion and coughs.
Menthone is good for colds because it has natural decongestant properties that help to clear mucus and ease congestion in the nose and sinuses.
Rosmarinic acid helps fight colds because it has strong antioxidant properties that reduce inflammation and kill the germs that cause illness.
Linalool is good for colds because it has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce congestion, fight off infections, and ease symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oil, also known as peppermint oil, is a natural remedy that can help relieve symptoms of the common cold. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can ease congestion and sore throats. When inhaled, peppermint oil's cooling sensation can help open up airways, making it easier to breathe. Its expectorant properties can also help loosen and clear out mucus, reducing coughs and congestion. Additionally, peppermint oil has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off the underlying infection that causes colds, promoting a faster recovery. By using peppermint oil in a diffuser or applying it topically with a carrier oil, you can experience its benefits and start to feel better when you're under the weather.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils that help with colds, start by drying Ginkgo biloba leaves in a warm, dry place away from direct sunlight. Once dry, crush the leaves into small pieces and mix them with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1 part Ginkgo biloba to 5 parts carrier oil. Let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container every few days, allowing the Ginkgo biloba to infuse into the carrier oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids and transfer the Ginkgo biloba essential oil to a dark glass bottle. This oil can be used to help alleviate cold symptoms, such as congestion and coughs, by inhaling it directly or adding a few drops to a diffuser. It's essential to note that the quality and effectiveness of the oil may vary depending on the source and quality of the Ginkgo biloba leaves, and it's always best to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for colds

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oil, also known as ginger oil, is a natural remedy for colds. It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the throat and nose, making it easier to breathe and swallow. The oil also has antibacterial properties that help fight off infections that cause colds. When inhaled, ginger oil can help ease congestion and open up airways, relieving a stuffy nose and cough. Additionally, ginger oil has natural expectorant properties that help bring up mucus and phlegm, making it easier to clear out the lungs. Ginger oil also has a warming effect that can help increase blood flow and circulation, which can help boost the immune system and fight off the underlying cause of a cold.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil that helps with colds, you'll need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and a few basic supplies. First, dry the Ginkgo biloba leaves completely by spreading them out in a single layer on a paper bag or a clean cloth. Next, place the dried leaves in a clean glass jar and cover them with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil. Let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally, allowing the Ginkgo biloba's active compounds to infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Finally, use a small amount of the Ginkgo biloba oil in a diffuser or apply it to your skin (after diluting it with another oil, like coconut or jojoba oil) to help alleviate cold symptoms, such as congestion and coughs.

When used in aromatherapy, Salvia officinalis essential oil can also help to ease a sore throat and reduce stress, making it easier to recover from a cold.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hyptis suaveolens essential oil is often used to help with colds. This oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and pain in the nose, sinuses, and throat. The oil's decongestant properties can ease congestion and make it easier to breathe. It also has antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off the underlying infection causing the cold. Additionally, Hyptis suaveolens oil has expectorant properties, which can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways. This can make it easier to cough up mucus and feel more comfortable when congested. When inhaled, the oil's vapors can also help to calm the body and reduce stress, which can help alleviate symptoms of a cold.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil that helps with colds, you will need some basic equipment and ingredients. First, you'll need to dry and process the Ginkgo biloba leaves. Ginkgo biloba leaves can be dried in a low-temperature oven (150°F - 200°F) or by air-drying them, making sure they are completely dry and crumbly. Next, use a coffee grinder or a mortar and pestle to finely chop the dried leaves into a powder. You'll then need to add the powder to a carrier oil, such as coconut or jojoba oil, in a ratio of 1 part Ginkgo biloba powder to 5 parts carrier oil. After that, store the mixture in a dark glass bottle and let it infuse for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the bottle daily. Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, and discard the solids. Your Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil is now ready to use. To help with colds, you can inhale the oil directly from the bottle, or add a few drops to a diffuser or a warm bath to open up your airways and ease congestion.
remedies.image_alt: Hyptis suaveolens herbal essential oils for colds

Caryophyllene is good for colds because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off the underlying causes of colds.
Borneol is good for colds because it has natural decongestant properties that help to reduce nasal congestion and relieve stuffiness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as true lavender, is a popular natural remedy for colds. The essential oil extracted from its flowers contains compounds that help to ease congestion and coughs. When inhaled, it can thin mucus and make it easier to expel, providing relief from stuffy noses and chests. Additionally, lavender oil's antimicrobial properties can help fight off bacteria and viruses that cause colds. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce swelling in the throat and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Furthermore, the calming effects of lavender oil can help promote restful sleep, which is essential for the body to recover from colds. By inhaling lavender oil through steam inhalation or using it in a diffuser, you can help alleviate cold symptoms and support your body's natural healing process.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil, you'll need a few basic supplies and some patience. First, gather Ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers, either by growing them yourself or purchasing them from a trusted herbal supplier. Next, clean and dry the leaves and flowers thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris. Then, place the dried leaves and flowers in a clean glass jar and cover them with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil. The ratio of leaves to oil is usually around 1:3, so for every part Ginkgo biloba, use three parts carrier oil. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to allow the oil to infuse. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, and discard the solids. This Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil can then be used to help alleviate cold symptoms, such as congestion and coughs, by inhaling it directly from the bottle or adding a few drops to a warm bath or vaporizer.

Ginkgolide B is good for colds because it helps block the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body, reducing congestion and relieving symptoms of colds and flu.
Kaempferol is good for colds because it has strong antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and fight off the viruses that cause colds.
Isorhapontigenin is good for colds because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and recover from a cold.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oil is often used to help with cold symptoms. The oil comes from the leaves of the ginkgo biloba tree, a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for a long time. It has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the nose and sinuses. This can make it easier to breathe and relieve congestion. The oil also has antiviral properties, which can help fight off the viruses that cause colds. Additionally, ginkgo biloba essential oil can help improve blood flow to the face and sinuses, which can help loosen mucus and promote drainage. This can make it easier to expel mucus and other debris from the body, helping to clear up a cold. Some people also find that it helps reduce coughing and sore throats associated with colds.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils that help with colds, you will need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, a clean glass jar, and a solvent like jojoba oil or grapeseed oil. First, dry the Ginkgo biloba leaves completely to prevent any moisture from affecting the oil. Then, fill the glass jar about one-third full with the dried leaves. Next, pour the solvent over the leaves, making sure that the leaves are completely covered. Now, let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar gently every day to help the solvent extract the medicinal properties from the leaves. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the solids. Finally, transfer the extracted oil to a smaller glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place, ready to use as a natural remedy to help alleviate cold symptoms when inhaled or applied topically.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils for colds

intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a good remedy for colds because they contain natural compounds that help ease symptoms. Eucalyptus globulus oil, for example, is known for its decongestant properties, which means it can help relieve stuffy noses and ease breathing. This is because eucalyptus oil contains a compound called eucalyptol, which can break down mucus and open up airways. Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme oil, has antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections, which can cause colds. This is because thyme oil contains compounds that can kill bacteria and other microorganisms that cause illness.  Fixing the issue of colds is important because it can help improve our quality of life. When we're sick with a cold, we often feel tired, congested, and miserable. This can make it difficult to go about our daily activities, which can impact our work, relationships, and overall well-being. By using herbal essential oils like eucalyptus and thyme, we can help alleviate symptoms and feel better faster. This can also help reduce the risk of complications from colds, such as ear infections and bronchitis. By taking proactive steps to manage colds, we can improve our health and well-being, and get back to living our normal lives.
supplementary_best: The best combination of herbal essential oils to remedy colds is a blend of Eucalyptus globulus, Melaleuca alternifolia, and Zingiber officinale. Eucalyptus globulus oil has decongestant properties, helping to clear mucus from the nose and sinuses. Melaleuca alternifolia oil, also known as tea tree oil, has antimicrobial properties, which help combat the infection that causes colds. Zingiber officinale oil, or ginger oil, has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and pain in the throat and body. This combination works well together because Eucalyptus globulus oil helps loosen mucus, while Melaleuca alternifolia oil kills the bacteria that cause the infection, and Zingiber officinale oil reduces the discomfort and pain associated with the infection.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a cold, there are some herbal essential oils you should avoid using.  Peppermint oil (Mentha piperita) can be too strong and irritate your throat and nasal passages, making your symptoms worse. It can also cause a burning sensation when inhaled directly.  Oregano oil (Origanum vulgare) is another oil to avoid when you have a cold. Its strong antimicrobial properties can be too intense for your body, causing stomach problems, headaches, and allergic reactions.  Tea tree oil (Melaleuca alternifolia) is not suitable for colds, as it can irritate your skin and respiratory system. It's best to avoid using it on your skin or in a diffuser when you're unwell.  Geranium oil (Pelargonium graveolens) may not be suitable for everyone with a cold, as it can trigger allergies and cause skin irritation in some people.  Sage oil (Salvia officinalis) is not listed in the banned list but it should be avoided due to its drying properties which can dehydrate you further when you are sick.  Cinnamon oil (Cinnamomum verum) is another oil to avoid when you have a cold. Its strong, spicy scent can irritate your throat and nasal passages, and its antimicrobial properties can be too intense for your body.  Ginger oil (Zingiber officinale) is also not suitable for colds, as it can irritate your skin and digestive system.
supplementary_other_remedies: When we get a cold, our body needs help to fight off the infection.  One natural remedy is honey, which is a thick, sweet liquid made by bees. Honey has antibacterial properties that can help kill the germs causing our cold. We can use it as a cough syrup to soothe our throat and calm the cough. Another remedy is garlic, which is a strong-smelling plant that has antiviral properties. We can chop it up and add it to our food or make a tea by boiling it in water. The heat from the water releases the active ingredients that help fight off the infection.  Slippery elm is a type of tree bark that can help soothe our throat and reduce inflammation. We can make a tea by boiling the bark in water and then drink it to help calm the cough and sore throat. Elderberry is a type of fruit that has antiviral properties, and we can use it to make a syrup or drink it as a juice to help fight off the infection. The heat from our body helps break down the active ingredients in the elderberry, which then helps to reduce the severity of our cold symptoms.  Epsom salt baths are another natural remedy that can help with colds. The high levels of magnesium in the Epsom salt help to relax our body and reduce inflammation, which can help to ease our cold symptoms. When we take an Epsom salt bath, the magnesium is absorbed through our skin and helps to calm our body and reduce the severity of our cold. Chicken soup is also a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to help with colds. The steam from the hot soup helps to loosen up the mucus in our chest, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.
supplementary_related_ailments: When you have a cold, you may feel more than just a sore throat and runny nose. You may also experience headaches, due to sinus pressure and inflammation. This is where feverfew comes in - its natural anti-inflammatory properties help reduce pain and swelling.  You might also feel tired and weak, which is often a result of your body fighting off the virus. Ginseng is known to boost energy levels and help your body recover faster. Its adaptogenic properties allow it to balance your immune system and reduce stress.  Some people with colds may experience coughing and congestion, making it hard to breathe. Thyme is a natural expectorant that helps loosen mucus and clear your airways. Its antibacterial properties also help fight off any secondary infections.  Sinus pressure and congestion can lead to a sore throat, which can be painful and make swallowing difficult. Slippery elm is a natural demulcent that soothes and protects your throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort.  You may also experience body aches and pains, due to your body's immune response. Willow bark contains salicin, a natural pain-reliever similar to aspirin. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce swelling and discomfort.  Elderberry is a natural antiviral that helps reduce the severity and duration of colds. It contains flavonoids and anthocyanins that help block the virus's ability to multiply and spread. This can help your body recover faster and reduce the risk of complications.  Remember to consult a healthcare professional before trying any new remedies, especially if you have underlying health conditions or take prescription medications.
